Consumer prices rose 3.3% in March, as energy prices spiked due to Iran conflict
headlines.desc: The consumer price index was expected to show a 3.3% year-over-year gain in March, according to the Dow Jones consensus.

headlines.title: China industrial profits surge 15% to start year, but oil price shock threatens outlook
headlines.desc: Soaring energy prices are expected to impact China's economy less than most other countries, due to its massive oil reserves and alternative energy sources.

headlines.title: Offbeat Wall Street research firm says it sent an analyst to Strait of Hormuz. Here's what they learned
headlines.desc: Before the U.S and Israel began their war against Iran, about 20% of the world's oil was shipped through the narrow Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Hormuz blockade could deepen world’s worst energy crisis — and risk a dangerous misstep
headlines.desc: The U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz threatens to halt more tanker traffic, sending oil surging and risks drawing China into a widening confrontation with Washington.

headlines.title: New fees, fewer flights: Higher fuel prices pinch consumer budgets beyond the gas pump
headlines.desc: As corporate policies change, Americans will feel higher oil prices in their wallets beyond the gas pump.

headlines.title: China suppliers warn of higher prices for Americans due to Strait of Hormuz closure
headlines.desc: The Iran war has stalled shipments of oil and its products through the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns among Chinese manufacturers about supply chain disruptions.

headlines.title: Elizabeth Warren demands answers on costs, economic impact of 'illegal and reckless war'
headlines.desc: The liberal Democratic firebrand ripped President Donald Trump, whom she said has 'dragged the United States into an illegal and reckless war.'

headlines.title: Top Wall Street analysts see strong growth potential in these 3 stocks
headlines.desc: Investors should look beyond short-term volatility and consider these growth stocks that analysts see upside for, according to TipRanks.

headlines.title: Top Wall Street analysts prefer these dividend stocks for steady income
headlines.desc: Given ongoing uncertainty, investors may seek to add some stability to their portfolios by buying dividend stocks for steady income.

headlines.title: Top Wall Street analysts like these dividend stocks for solid returns
headlines.desc: These stocks that pay dividends consistently have the ability to generate long-term capital appreciation.

headlines.title: Iran oil shock stirs memories of 1997 Asian Financial Crisis — but here’s why history may not repeat itself
headlines.desc: The Hormuz disruption is hammering Asian economies with surging energy costs and weakening currencies, but a repeat of the crisis three decades ago is unlikely.

headlines.title: Inflation held sticky at 3% as U.S. headed into war with Iran, key Fed gauge shows
headlines.desc: The gauge released Thursday offers the Federal Reserve a snapshot of conditions leading into the Iran war.

headlines.title: Is Tesla a chip stock now? Investors are cheering a semiconductor milestone.
headlines.desc: The AI5 chip, which Tesla says will power humanoid robots and supercomputers, has completed a critical step.

headlines.title: Curry scores 35 as Warriors upset Clippers to extend playoff run
headlines.desc: Stephen Curry's Golden State Warriors keep their NBA playoff hopes alive, eliminating LA Clippers from the play-in.

headlines.title: Traders now see little chance of an interest rate cut this year following Fed decision
headlines.desc: All of the positive economic talk out of this week's Federal Reserve meeting had a negative impact on investors

headlines.title: Wholesale prices rose 0.5% in March, much less than expected despite war impact
headlines.desc: The producer price index was expected to increase 1.1% in March, according to the Dow Jones consensus estimate.

headlines.title: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
headlines.desc: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

headlines.title: Warren Buffett says he sold Apple too soon and would buy more of it, though not in this market
headlines.desc: Apple remains Berkshire Hathaway's largest holding even after the conglomerate trimmed its stake at the end of last year.

headlines.title: Why $4 a gallon gas prices won’t trigger Fed interest rate hikes — and could lead to cuts
headlines.desc: While there's still plenty of uncertainty about where rates are headed, Wall Street commentary shifted back to expectations for cuts.
